candidate_lesson.lesson_text: When the 30-indicator consensus shows conv=100% but green_matches=0 and hist_hr=0% (USDJPY LONG, AUDNZD LONG), the signal is a directional artifact with no historical precedent — treat conv=100%/hist_hr=0% pairs as UNRELIABLE and veto, regardless of indicator agreement.
candidate_lesson.mechanism: A 100% conviction reading with zero historically-matching green setups means the indicator stack has never co-aligned this way in a winning configuration — it is an out-of-distribution state, not a strong signal. Institutionally, USDJPY LONG and AUDNZD LONG here are safe-haven/carry-driven, and per L8 rising VIX (17.28→19.49) corrupts the rate signal exactly when consensus looks most confident.
candidate_lesson.testable_rule: if consensus_conv == 1.0 and consensus_hist_hr == 0.0 and green_matches == 0: return Veto('OOD_consensus_no_precedent')
candidate_lesson.expected_impact: Avoids zero-precedent entries on USDJPY/AUDNZD; expected to remove negative-tail trades and lift Sharpe ~+0.02 by trimming variance from unprecedented configurations.
candidate_lesson.evidence_from_harvest: Live consensus: USDJPY 16/30 bull conv=100% hist_hr=0% green_matches=0; AUDNZD 15/30 bull conv=100% hist_hr=0% green_matches=0 — both contrast sharply with GBPUSD/EURUSD/AUDUSD SHORT signals at hist_hr 66-72%.

retirement_flag.reason: VIX has risen from 17.28 to 19.49 across two pulses; if the 13 gate is now vetoing all USDJPY/AUDNZD flow continuously, the rule may be over-firing and should be checked against whether any safe-haven trades are still surviving the gate.

reflection.system_health_note: Regime UNKNOWN with 0 trades in 7 days and TradingView showing 0 signals — system is in a low-conviction internal-only state at 75% sizing, so any consensus showing 100% conv with 0% historical hit rate should be treated as suspect rather than actionable.
